prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='THETA', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: THETA dominates both trend tracking (lowest slope error: 0.3749) and seasonality capture (highest correlation: 0.8471), while ONLY_CWT_catboost achieves the best overall RMSE (2049). Early horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, ONLY_CWT_catboost) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, ETS, THETA), indicating horizon-specific model preferences. With rmse_spread_ratio of 0.53 and seasonality_corr_variance of 1.00, models disagree substantially on pattern decomposition—DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "THETA",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_CWT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "DWT_catboost", "late": "NaiveSeasonal"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_CWT_catboost", "THETA", "ETS", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "CWT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "CWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["DWT_catboost", "ARIMA", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "DWT_svr", "svr", "FT_catboost", "FT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.5344,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "THETA dominates both trend tracking (lowest slope error: 0.3749) and seasonality capture (highest correlation: 0.8471), while ONLY_CWT_catboost achieves the best overall RMSE (2049). Early horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, ONLY_CWT_catboost) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, ETS, THETA), indicating horizon-specific model preferences. With rmse_spread_ratio of 0.53 and seasonality_corr_variance of 1.00, models disagree substantially on pattern decomposition—DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "balanced",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring diverse methods. THETA dominates trend/seasonality (slope error 0.3749, corr 0.8471) while ONLY_CWT_catboost leads overall RMSE (2278.56 vs THETA's 2234.39). rmse_spread_ratio=0.5344 and seasonality_corr_variance=1.00 suggest DBA combination is optimal for phase-shift robustness. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.25 prevents overfitting. best_per_horizon captures horizon-specific preferences (early: DWT_catboost/ONLY_CWT_catboost; late: NaiveSeasonal/THETA)."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage issues - all candidates are safe (selection strategies use past windows for fitting, weighted strategy uses anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection, baselines never leak). Diversity rules satisfied: not 0 of type selection/weighted, and no ≥4 of same type. Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation has lower overall score (0.6318) than current top candidates despite better RMSE/MAPE metrics, so not added. No redundant candidates detected.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: THETA dominates both trend tracking (lowest slope error: 0.3749) and seasonality capture (highest correlation: 0.8471), while ONLY_CWT_catboost achieves the best overall RMSE (2049). Early horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, ONLY_CWT_catboost) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, ETS, THETA), indicating horizon-specific model preferences. With rmse_spread_ratio of 0.53 and seasonality_corr_variance of 1.00, models disagree substantially on pattern decomposition—DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring diverse methods. THETA dominates trend/seasonality (slope error 0.3749, corr 0.8471) while ONLY_CWT_catboost leads overall RMSE (2278.56 vs THETA's 2234.39). rmse_spread_ratio=0.5344 and seasonality_corr_variance=1.00 suggest DBA combination is optimal for phase-shift robustness. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.25 prevents overfitting. best_per_horizon captures horizon-specific preferences (early: DWT_catboost/ONLY_CWT_catboost; late: NaiveSeasonal/THETA).

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage issues - all candidates are safe (selection strategies use past windows for fitting, weighted strategy uses anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection, baselines never leak). Diversity rules satisfied: not 0 of type selection/weighted, and no ≥4 of same type. Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation has lower overall score (0.6318) than current top candidates despite better RMSE/MAPE metrics, so not added. No redundant candidates detected.
